title: Daily Source Digest
note: Daily source-balanced news digest — morning update.

stories.title: Trump Announces $10 Billion Defense Boom, Thousands Of New Jobs
stories.summary:
Trump Announces $10 Billion Defense Boom, Thousands Of New Jobs. President Donald Trump announced a $10 billion War Department investment in materiel production Wednesday, saying the funding is expected to support more than 4,000 defense-industry jobs across Pennsylvania.

Trump made the announcement during keynote remarks at the first Pennsylvania Defense and Innovation Summit at the U.S. Army War College in Carlisle, Pennsylvania. The two-day event ...

Breitbart: Trump's Defense Manufacturing Boom in Pennsylvania Takes Center Stage at McCormick's War College Summit.

Breitbart reports that titans of the defense and tech spheres descended on the U.S. Army War College in Carlisle, Pennsylvania, this week for Sen. Dave McCormick's (R-PA) Defense & Innovation Summit, where President Donald Trump's and McCormick's defense manufacturing boom in the commonwealth was on full display.

stories.title: Trump alleges China meddled in 2020 election and questions voting security ahead of midterms
stories.summary:
Trump alleges China meddled in 2020 election and questions voting security ahead of midterms. China said Trump's claims were fabricated and US Democrats said he was paving the way to undermine November's elections.

Al Jazeera: China slams Trump’s claims over 2020 US election interference.

Al Jazeera reports that china has responded to US President Donald Trump’s claims that China interfered in the 2020 US presidential election.

stories.title: Uganda suspends school trips after 20 pupils die in bus crash
stories.summary:
Uganda suspends school trips after 20 pupils die in bus crash. Thousands are killed on Uganda's roads each year but this is one of its deadliest accidents involving children.

Al Jazeera: Uganda school bus crash kills 20 children and 1 adult.

Al Jazeera reports that uganda halts school trips after bus crashes returning from waterfall field trip; at least a dozen also injured.

stories.title: Markwayne Mullin says ICE is ‘turning up the heat on the streets’ after traffic stop turnaround
stories.summary:
Markwayne Mullin says ICE is ‘turning up the heat on the streets’ after traffic stop turnaround. Homeland Security Secretary Markwayne Mullin said Immigration and Customs Enforcement plans to “turn up the heat” with traffic stops following confusion with the White House about whether vehicle stops would continue.

Mullin hosted a press conference on Friday meant to focus on election security, but the department head faced questions about ICE operations following two […]. Daily Wire: Mullin Won’t Say Whether He Briefed Trump Before Pausing ICE Traffic Stops.

Daily Wire reports that homeland Security Secretary Markwayne Mullin won’t say whether he briefed President Donald Trump before briefly pausing Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) traffic stops in the wake of two recent fatal shootings.

During a Friday briefing on the department’s efforts to clean up the voter rolls of nonciti

stories.title: ‘Sex and the City’ Actress Appointed To Help Pick NYC Judges
stories.summary:
‘Sex and the City’ Actress Appointed To Help Pick NYC Judges. “Sex and the City” actress Cynthia Nixon has been appointed to screen New York judges despite having no legal training or experience whatsoever.

The leftist activist and failed gubernatorial candidate was just appointed to serve on the Commission on Judicial Nomination by New York Chief Judge Rowan Wilson, as the New York Post first reported. ...

Breitbart: 'Sex and the City' Star Cynthia Nixon Will Screen, Recommend Candidates to Serve on New York's Highest Court. Breitbart reports that actress Cynthia Nixon, who played a lawyer on HBO's "Sex and the City," will now be screening and recommending candidates to serve on the state of New York's highest court.

The post ‘Sex and the City’ Star Cynthia Nixon Will Screen, Recommend Candidates to Serve on New York’s Highest Court appeared firs.

stories.title: Is Trump going to stop treating China with kid gloves now?
stories.summary:
Is Trump going to stop treating China with kid gloves now?. President Donald Trump leveled a series of accusations on Thursday that China interfered in the 2020 election to prevent him from winning.

Is that going to be enough for him to stop treating China with kid gloves and start treating it like the American enemy it is?

Trump claimed that “in mid-2018, China was working to influence […]. Washington Times: China fumes at Trump as a new rift opens between the rival superpowers.

Washington Times reports that china is reacting angrily to President Trump's claims that it stole information about 220 million American voters during the 2020 election, dubbing them "groundless accusations.".

stories.title: Mark Carney Deflects Blame on Canada Wildfire Failures: 'Climate Change Is Everyone's Responsibility'
stories.summary: Mark Carney Deflects Blame on Canada Wildfire Failures: 'Climate Change Is Everyone's Responsibility'. Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney dismissed the possibility that his government could be doing more to contain the out-of-control wildfires ravaging Ontario this week and sending toxic smoke down to flood much of the American Midwest and Northeast, demanding the United States do more to fight alleged "climate change." stories.title: Trump's speech a desperate move — and 'ominous sign' ahead of midterms
stories.summary:
Trump's speech a desperate move — and 'ominous sign' ahead of midterms. Using a prime-time address to the nation to repeat long-debunked claims about the 2020 election defeat is a desperate and unusual move.

Analysts say it's an "ominous sign" of how Donald Trump may handle the upcoming midterms.

stories.title: Jana Armstrong and her sister made a pact to care for each other’s children should the worst happen. Then it did
stories.summary:
Jana Armstrong and her sister made a pact to care for each other’s children should the worst happen. Then it did.

Faith Issacs knew her big sister was in terrible trouble a few days before her body was found – because nothing would have kept her from her infant son Sisters Jana Armstrong and Faith Isaacs were supposed to raise their children together.

But just a few weeks ago, in the shock of grief, the young mothers made a pact. If one should die before her time, the other would raise her sister’s son.

“Just because Jana and I are quite well aware of how unexpected life can be,” Faith says.

But just a few weeks ago, in the shock of grief, the young mothers made a pact. If one should die before her time, the other would raise her sister’s son. “Just because Jana and I are quite well aware of how unexpected life can be,” Faith says.

stories.title: No product? No problem. This Disrupt 2026 session shows how to get pre-seed funding with conviction, storytelling
stories.summary:
No product? No problem.

This Disrupt 2026 session shows how to get pre-seed funding with conviction, storytelling. It’s not just you: AI startups are taking in a huge amount of seed funding, and in the process making things harder for anyone looking for funding even at a pre-seed stage.

stories.title: ‘Adversarial clothing’: are garments designed to confuse facial recognition systems about to go mainstream?
stories.summary:
‘Adversarial clothing’: are garments designed to confuse facial recognition systems about to go mainstream?.

Designers say that as well as offering a degree of protection from surveillance, their clothes make a powerful fashion statement about the importance of privacy As facial recognition technology is rolled out across Britain’s public spaces, a new generation of designers say privacy could be the next big fashion trend.

Companies have started incorporating “adversarial patterns” in their garments – carefully designed arrangements of shapes, colours and repeated motifs said to exploit.

links.headline: ‘Adversarial clothing’: are garments designed to confuse facial recognition systems about to go mainstream?

stories.summary:
DepEd, CHED, Tesda launch One Education to accelerate education, reforms.

stories.title: Tolosa: High ratings no shield from impeachment accountability
stories.summary:
Tolosa: High ratings no shield from impeachment accountability. MANILA, Philippines – Vice President Sara Duterte’s improved satisfaction rating cannot halt or weaken her impeachment trial because constitutional accountability is decided on evidence, not popularity.

House prosecution legal spokesperson and private prosecutor Benjamin “Jay” Tolosa Jr. stressed that public approval does not place any impeachable official beyond scrutiny for alleged violations of the Constitution. Philippine Daily Inquirer: Stop impeachment amid Duterte ratings?

This ain’t a pageant – Barbers.

stories.summary:

stories.summary:
Missing sabungeros’ kin deny bribes from Atong Ang camp. MANILA, Philippines – Several relatives of the missing sabungeros (cockfighting enthusiasts) have revealed that Julie Patidongan has been egging them to extract money from the camp of businessman Charlie “Atong” Ang.

stories.title: PDEA-BARMM agents dismantle drug den, arrest 6
stories.summary:
PDEA-BARMM agents dismantle drug den, arrest 6. COTABATO CITY – Anti-narcotics agents dismantled a drug den and arrested six suspects, including a woman, during a buy-bust operation in Midsayap, Cotabato province on Thursday, July 16.

Director Edgar T.

Jubay of Philippine Drug Enforcement Agency in the Bangsamoro Autonomous Region in Muslim Mindanao (PDEA-BARMM), said the buy-bust operation was conducted in Barangay Poblacion. Philippine Daily Inquirer: PDEA, cops dismantle alleged drug den in Rizal; 5 arrested.

Philippine Daily Inquirer reports that lUCENA CITY – Agents of the Philippine Drug Enforcement Agency (PDEA), assisted by police, dismantled an alleged drug den and arrested five suspects in Binangonan, Rizal, early on Friday.

stories.title: Patreon stops asking AI bots not to scrape — and starts blocking them
stories.summary:
Patreon stops asking AI bots not to scrape — and starts blocking them. Patreon is strengthening its defenses against AI scraping by working with Cloudflare to block bots that train AI models on creators’ content without permission.

The move marks a shift away from relying on websites using robots.txt alone to actively block unauthorized AI training.

stories.title: Denshattack! review – time to get on board with kickflipping trains
stories.summary:
Denshattack! review – time to get on board with kickflipping trains. PC, PS5, Xbox Series X/S, Nintendo Switch 2; Undercoders Colourful, counter-cultural and captivating – this rail riding game set in a dystopian Japan is as weird as it is exhilarating Every now and again a game appears with a premise so outrageous you stop in your tracks to take it all in.

Denshattack!, a game about kickflipping trains across a dystopian future Japan, is the epitome of this feeling. Set in a post climate disaster world, people have retreated to corpo.

stories.title: Robert Laidlow: Reality Eaters album review
stories.summary:
Robert Laidlow: Reality Eaters album review. BBC Philharmonic/Havlat/Kaziboni/Piatti Quartet (NMC) Einstein’s field equations, Newton’s universal law and artificial intelligence are among the subjects of Laidlow’s ambitious orchestral works Robert Laidlow is as at home in the realms of science and technology as he is in the world of classical music.

As this NMC debut album demonstrates, his intricate, wildly imaginative work is eminently approachable, even if the core concepts are highly complex. Warp,.

stories.title: Trump rails against New York’s statewide datacenter moratorium
stories.summary:
Trump rails against New York’s statewide datacenter moratorium.

AI-friendly president shared a post saying governor Kathy Hochul should scrap the one-year policy ‘IMMEDIATELY’ Donald Trump railed against the New York governor, Kathy Hochul, for pausing the construction of large new datacenters, the resource-intensive facilities that power artificial intelligence.

New York became the first US state to enact a moratorium on new datacenters on Tuesday, when Hochul signed an executive orde.

stories.summary:
